title: Pentecost Sunday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: May 19, 2024
Preacher: Katie Pearson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

Pentecost Sunday, the seventh Sunday of Easter and one of the three principal feasts of the Christian year, marking the descent of the Holy Spirit upon the apostles. The Revised Common Lectionary readings would have included Acts 2:1-21 (the Pentecost narrative), Psalm 104:24-34, Romans 8:22-27, and John 15:26-27 or 16:4-15 (Jesus's promise of the Advocate). This falls in the Season of Easter, specifically at its climactic conclusion, with the liturgical color transitioning to red for the feast day.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the period following General Convention 2024 (held in Louisville in June would be upcoming), so diocesan preparations were underway; the Diocese of Colorado would have been focused on spring confirmations and ordinations. Bishop Paula Clark was serving as Bishop of Colorado at this time, having been elected in 2022. No major denominational controversies or elections were in immediate focus on this specific date, though the Church remained engaged with ongoing debates about sexuality and inclusion that had characterized recent years.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was in late spring weather typical for May, with mild temperatures ideal for the city's outdoor community events. The Denver Broncos' 2024 NFL Draft had occurred on April 25, keeping the city engaged in football season anticipation. Capitol Hill and downtown Denver were active with spring community events, though no major weather emergencies or local political crises dominated the immediate week before this sermon.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

President Biden's campaign was in full swing with the general election six months away, though no major breakthroughs dominated the week of May 12-18. The Supreme Court had recently issued its final decisions of the term (late June), but mid-May saw standard political coverage and primary election wrap-up. The US economy was a persistent topic with inflation concerns continuing despite Federal Reserve interest rate actions earlier in the year.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

Russia's continued invasion of Ukraine remained an ongoing humanitarian and geopolitical crisis, with Ukraine conducting ongoing military operations and facing significant destruction. The situation in Gaza following the October 2023 Hamas attack and subsequent Israeli military response was a major ongoing international story with significant humanitarian concerns. Tensions between the US/NATO and Russia over Ukraine aid and military support remained active diplomatic and military concerns.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in May 2024 was in the throes of heightened political polarization with the presidential election consuming significant national attention, creating an underlying tension in many communities. The Denver Nuggets' 2024 NBA season had concluded in the playoffs (losing to Minnesota in the second round in May), deflating some of the euphoria from their 2023 championship. Late spring offered a general cultural shift toward outdoor activities, graduations, and wedding season planning, with streaming entertainment and social media discourse reflecting typical pre-summer cultural fragmentation.
